summary: High‑energy Boston Garden set with soaring Samson & Delilah and tight jams
blurb: The band rides a relentless groove through Jack Straw, Sugaree and West L.A., then detonates in a blistering second set highlighted by Samson & Delilah. The encore’s tender Baby Blue caps an evening of pure Dead fire.
review: September 18, 1982 at the Boston Garden finds the Dead locked in a high‑octane groove that still feels fresh decades later. The first set rolls out classic road‑trip fare—Jack Straw into Sugaree, a rollicking Mexicali Blues segue and a bright West L.A. Fadeaway—that sets a buoyant tone for the night. The second set is where the magic peaks: Samson & Delilah erupts with Jerry’s “smokin’ strings” and Brent’s piano lift on Uncle John’s Band/Playing In The Band, while Phil drops a “bomb‑justice” bass line that drives the band forward. A thunderous drum solo and an explosive Throwing Stones (the first Boston Garden performance) keep the momentum roaring into The Wheel and Truckin’. The show closes with a heartfelt It’s All Over Now Baby Blue that showcases Jerry’s voice at its warmest. Across all available soundboard and audience tapes the performance shines, even if some SBD mixes feel a touch thin on bass; the energy and tightness of the band more than compensate, making this a must‑listen for any Deadhead looking for an ’80s high point.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven of eight recordings are uniformly positive, praising the band’s energy, standout songs (Samson & Delilah, Jack Straw, Throwing Stones) and strong performances; only one mixed review notes a thin bass but does not detract from overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard with clear guitar, vocals and balanced mix; captures the high‑energy peaks (Samson & Delilah, Baby Blue) while retaining the live vibe.

key_highlights: Blazing second‑set energy culminating in Samson & Delilah
First‑set classics Jack Straw, Sugaree, West L.A. Fadeaway flow seamlessly
Throwing Stones debut at Boston Garden adds historic punch
Tender Baby Blue encore showcases Jerry’s vocal warmth
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Smokin' strings and powerful lead tones dominate, with a heartfelt delivery on Baby Blue and consistently praised guitar work throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation; occasional thinness noted but highlighted by a ‘bomb‑justice’ line that drives Samson & Delilah forward.
band_performance.Bob: Lively banter and mouthy, ornery presence; dedicated Samson to the building and added memorable vocal chants that lifted the set.
band_performance.Brent: Strong piano work on Far From Me, Uncle John's Band and Playing In The Band; overall keyboard presence adds depth and sparkle.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful drum solo praised for its intensity; despite a ‘toy‑like’ SBD impression, his rhythmic drive fuels the high‑energy flow.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers tight, propulsive drumming that locks in with Mickey, keeping the groove relentless from first set through the encore.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
